system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

# Codebase Inspection with pygount

Analyze repositories for lines of code, language breakdown, file counts, and code-vs-comment ratios using `pygount`.

## When to Use

- User asks for LOC (lines of code) count
- User wants a language breakdown of a repo
- User asks about codebase size or composition
- User wants code-vs-comment ratios
- General "how big is this repo" questions

## Prerequisites

```bash
pip install --break-system-packages pygount 2>/dev/null || pip install pygount
```

## 1. Basic Summary (Most Common)

Get a full language breakdown with file counts, code lines, and comment lines:

```bash
cd /path/to/repo
pygount --format=summary \
  --folders-to-skip=".git,node_modules,venv,.venv,__pycache__,.cache,dist,build,.next,.tox,.eggs,*.egg-info" \
  .
```

**IMPORTANT:** Always use `--folders-to-skip` to exclude dependency/build directories, otherwise pygount will crawl them and take a very long time or hang.

## 2. Common Folder Exclusions

Adjust based on the project type:

```bash
# Python projects
--folders-to-skip=".git,venv,.venv,__pycache__,.cache,dist,build,.tox,.eggs,.mypy_cache"

# JavaScript/TypeScript projects
--folders-to-skip=".git,node_modules,dist,build,.next,.cache,.turbo,coverage"

# General catch-all
--folders-to-skip=".git,node_modules,venv,.venv,__pycache__,.cache,dist,build,.next,.tox,vendor,third_party"
```

## 3. Filter by Specific Language

```bash
# Only count Python files
pygount --suffix=py --format=summary .

# Only count Python and YAML
pygount --suffix=py,yaml,yml --format=summary .
```

## 4. Detailed File-by-File Output

```bash
# Default format shows per-file breakdown
pygount --folders-to-skip=".git,node_modules,venv" .

# Sort by code lines (pipe through sort)
pygount --folders-to-skip=".git,node_modules,venv" . | sort -t$'\t' -k1 -nr | head -20
```

## 5. Output Formats

```bash
# Summary table (default recommendation)
pygount --format=summary .

# JSON output for programmatic use
pygount --format=json .

# Pipe-friendly: Language, file count, code, docs, empty, string
pygount --format=summary . 2>/dev/null
```

## 6. Interpreting Results

The summary table columns:
- **Language** — detected programming language
- **Files** — number of files of that language
- **Code** — lines of actual code (executable/declarative)
- **Comment** — lines that are comments or documentation
- **%** — percentage of total

Special pseudo-languages:
- `__empty__` — empty files
- `__binary__` — binary files (images, compiled, etc.)
- `__generated__` — auto-generated files (detected heuristically)
- `__duplicate__` — files with identical content
- `__unknown__` — unrecognized file types

## Pitfalls

1. **Always exclude .git, node_modules, venv** — without `--folders-to-skip`, pygount will crawl everything and may take minutes or hang on large dependency trees.
2. **Markdown shows 0 code lines** — pygount classifies all Markdown content as comments, not code. This is expected behavior.
3. **JSON files show low code counts** — pygount may count JSON lines conservatively. For accurate JSON line counts, use `wc -l` directly.
4. **Large monorepos** — for very large repos, consider using `--suffix` to target specific languages rather than scanning everything.

# DepiFlash — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://depiflash.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/depiflash
- **Docker service:** depiflash_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`depiflash.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → depiflash_web:3000

## Pages
Home, Servicios, Cómo funciona, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad

## Design System
Coral/peach + lavender + teal. Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, professional, Spanish.

## Content
All content in `content/es.json`. Pricing, FAQ, prep instructions — all there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t depiflash:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml depiflash
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary booking channel
- Coral/teal gradient theme
- Mobile laser service — no fixed location

{"content": "     1|# DepiFlash — Business Plan & Market Analysis
     2|
     3|> Depilación láser IPL a domicilio en Asunción
     4|> Owner: trabaja en Uber durante el día
     5|> Fecha: [REDACTED_PHONE]
     6|
     7|---
     8|
     9|## 1. THE ADVANTAGE: UBER + LASER COMBO
    10|
    11|El dueño ya tiene el activo más importante: **el auto y el conocimiento de la ciudad**. No está empezando de cero.
    12|
    13|### Cómo se complementan
    14|
    15|| Uber | DepiFlash (Láser) |
    16||------|-------------------|
    17|| Maneja todo el día por Asunción | Sesiones de 15-45 min en domicilio |
    18|| Horarios pico (6-10am, 2-6pm, 9pm-12am) | Horarios valle (10am-2pm, 6-9pm) |
    19|| Ingreso bajo en horas valle | Ingreso alto justo en horas valle |
    20|| Conoce cada barrio y calle | Sabe dónde viven los clientes ideales |
    21|| Habla con 20-30 personas por día | Cada conversación puede generar un lead |
    22|
    23|No es "dejar Uber para hacer láser". Es **usar Uber como canal de marketing y llenar los huecos del día con láser**.
    24|
    25|---
    26|
    27|## 2. MERCADO EN PARAGUAY
    28|
    29|### Demanda
    30|- La depilación láser creció fuerte en PY últimos 5 años
    31|- Antes era solo para clase alta (clínicas caras)
    32|- Ahora hay demanda de clase media también
    33|- El problema: las clínicas son caras y quedan lejos
    34|- **Nadie ofrece a domicilio** — es un nicho vacío
    35|
    36|### Competencia en Asunción
    37|
    38|| Competidor | Tipo | Precio x sesión | Diferencia con nosotros |
    39||-----------|------|-----------------|------------------------|
    40|| Clínicas láser (Depilasé, Láser Medical, etc.) | Local fijo | Gs. 80,000-250,000 | Caro, hay que ir, pagan local |
    41|| Centros de estética (spa, peluquerías) | Local fijo | Gs. 100,000-200,000 | No tienen IPL, usan luz pulsada |
    42|| Particulares con máquina | A domicilio | NO HAY | **Nicho vacío** |
    43|
    44|**Ventaja:** No hay competencia directa en móvil.
    45|
    46|### Precios de Referencia en Asunción
    47|
    48|| Zona | Clínica (por sesión) | Nuestro precio sugerido | Clínica (6 sesiones) | Nuestro paquete 6 sesiones |
    49||------|---------------------|----------------------|---------------------|---------------------------|
    50|| Labio | Gs. 60,000-80,000 | Gs. 50,000 | Gs. 360,000 | Gs. 250,000 |
    51|| Axilas | Gs. 80,000-120,000 | Gs. 70,000 | Gs. 600,000 | Gs. 350,000 |
    52|| Bikini | Gs. 100,000-150,000 | Gs. 80,000 | Gs. 750,000 | Gs. 400,000 |
    53|| Media pierna | Gs. 120,000-180,000 | Gs. 100,000 | Gs. 900,000 | Gs. 500,000 |
    54|| Piernas completas | Gs. 200,000-350,000 | Gs. 180,000 | Gs. 1.500,000 | Gs. 900,000 |
    55|| Espalda | Gs. 250,000-400,000 | Gs. 200,000 | Gs. 1.800,000 | Gs. 1.000,000 |
    56|
    57|---
    58|
    59|## 3. TARGET AUDIENCE (A QUIÉN APUNTAR)
    60|
    61|### Perfil #1: Mujer 25-45, clase media-alta (PRINCIPAL)
    62|- Vive en: Villa Morra, Carmelitas, Recoleta, San Cristóbal, Las Lomas, Santa Teresa
    63|- Trabaja: oficina, profesional independiente, negocio propio
    64|- Problema: No tiene tiempo de ir a clínicas
    65|- Lo que valora: comodidad, privacidad, que vayan a su casa
    66|- Cómo llega: recomendación de amigas, Instagram, grupos de WhatsApp
    67|
    68|### Perfil #2: Hombre 25-40 (SECUNDARIO)
    69|- Vive en: mismos barrios
    70|- Le interesa: espalda, pecho, barba
    71|- Problema: Vergüenza de ir a clínicas de mujeres
    72|- Lo que valora: privacidad total
    73|- Cómo llega: boca a boca, Instagram
    74|
    75|### Perfil #3: Mamá joven 28-38 (TERCIARIO)
    76|- Con hijos chicos, no puede salir
    77|- Valora: que vayan a su casa mientras los hijos duermen
    78|- Zonas más comunes: bikini, axilas
    79|
    80|---
    81|
    82|## 4. ESTRATEGIA DE MARKETING
    83|
    84|### Canal #1: UBER (el más importante. Gratis.)
    85|
    86|| Táctica | Costo | Impacto |
    87||---------|-------|---------|
    88|| Tarjetas en el asiento trasero con QR a WhatsApp | Gs. 200,000/mes | ALTO — cada pasajero ve la tarjeta |
    89|| Conversación natural con pasajeras: "¿Conocés la depilación láser?" | $0 | MUY ALTO — confianza de Uber |
    90|| Descuento "Uber10" para primeros clientes | 10% desc. | Medio — incentiva probar |
    91|| Calcomanía chica en la ventana trasera | Gs. 50,000 | Medio — branding pasivo |
    92|
    93|**Guion para Uber:** 
    94|"— ¿Escuchaste hablar de la depilación láser IPL? Yo también hago eso, voy a la casa. Si querés probar, te dejo mi tarjeta."
    95|"— Tengo promo para pasajeros de Uber, 10% de descuento en la primera sesión."
    96|
    97|### Canal #2: REFERIDOS (el segundo más importante)
    98|- "Traé una amiga y las dos tienen 20% OFF"
    99|- "Referí a 3 amigas que compren y tenés 1 sesión gratis"
   100|- Darle 3 tarjetas a cada cliente para que reparta
   101|- Esto funciona MUY bien en Paraguay (cultura de recomendación)
   102|
   103|### Canal #3: INSTAGRAM
   104|- @depiflash.py (o @depiflash.ok)
   105|- Contenido: antes/después (con permiso), tips, promos
   106|- Ubicaciones: etiquetar barrios donde está disponible
   107|- Historias: "Estoy en Villa Morra todo el día, si querés sesión avisame"
   108|- Sin presupuesto de anuncios (orgánico)
   109|
   110|### Canal #4: WHATSAPP
   111|- Estados: "Hoy tengo 2 horarios libres en Carmelitas"
   112|- Listas de difusión: ofertas relámpago para same-day bookings
   113|- Catálogo de WhatsApp Business con precios
   114|
   115|### Canal #5: GOOGLE MY BUSINESS
   116|- Registrarse como "Depilación láser a domicilio"
   117|- Área de servicio: Asunción + Gran Asunción
   118|- Primeras 10 clientes → pedir reseña en Google
   119|
   120|### Canal #6: FACEBOOK GROUPS
   121|- Grupos de mujeres paraguayas, belleza, "Compro y Vendo PY"
   122|- Publicar 2x/semana: "Depilación láser IPL en tu casa ⚡ Primera sesión con descuento"
   123|
   124|---
   125|
   126|## 5. OPERACIONES (CÓMO TRABAJAR)
   127|
   128|### Compra de Máquina IPL
   129|- Buscar en: Facebook Marketplace Paraguay, grupos de "Importaciones PY"
   130|- Precio: Gs. 3,000,000 - 8,000,000
   131|- Buscar: "máquina IPL depilación"
   132|- Recomendado: IPL con cartucho de al menos 100,000 disparos
   133|- Verificar que tenga: certificación CE o FDA, modos de intensidad ajustables
   134|
   135|### Preparación
   136|1. Comprar la máquina IPL
   137|2. Comprar: gel conductor, lentes de seguridad (2 pares: uno para él, uno para la clienta)
   138|3. Hacer un curso online de IPL (YouTube es suficiente para empezar)
   139|4. Practicar en él mismo primero
   140|5. Sacar fotos de su propio antes/después para el Instagram
   141|
   142|### Logística de Sesiones
   143|- **Confirmar el día anterior:** "Confirmamos mañana a las 11am en tu casa?"
   144|- **Protocolo:** llamar, pasar, instalar máquina, sesión 15-45 min, cobrar, retirarse
   145|- **Pago:** efectivo o transferencia al momento
   146|- **Recordatorio:** mandar mensaje 24h antes recordando afeitar la zona
   147|
   148|### Rutina Diaria Ideal
   149|
   150|| Hora | Actividad |
   151||------|-----------|
   152|| 06:00 - 09:00 | Uber (hora pico mañana) |
   153|| 09:00 - 10:00 | Descanso, responder WhatsApp |
   154|| 10:00 - 12:00 | Sesiones láser (2-3) |
   155|| 12:00 - 14:00 | Uber (mediodía) |
   156|| 14:00 - 17:00 | Uber (tarde) |
   157|| 17:00 - 19:00 | Sesiones láser (2-3) |
   158|| 19:00 - 22:00 | Uber (noche) |
   159|| 22:00 - 23:00 | Responder WhatsApp, agendar próximo día |
   160|
   161|---
   162|
   163|## 6. FINANZAS
   164|
   165|### Inversión Inicial
   166|
   167|| Item | Costo |
   168||------|-------|
   169|| Máquina IPL | Gs. 4,000,000 |
   170|| Lentes de seguridad | Gs. 150,000 |
   171|| Gel conductor | Gs. 50,000 |
   172|| Tarjetas personales (500 unid) | Gs. 200,000 |
   173|| Calcomanía para auto | Gs. 50,000 |
   174|| **TOTAL** | **Gs. 4,450,000 (~$570)** |
   175|
   176|### Proyección de Ingresos
   177|
   178|| Escenario | Sesiones/día | Días/semana | Precio prom. | Ingreso mensual | Neto mensual |
   179||-----------|-------------|-------------|-------------|----------------|-------------|
   180|| **Mínimo (part-time)** | 2 | 5 | Gs. 100,000 | Gs. 4,000,000 | Gs. 3,500,000 |
   181|| **Moderado** | 4 | 6 | Gs. 120,000 | Gs. 11,500,000 | Gs. 10,800,000 |
   182|| **Full-time** | 6 | 6 | Gs. 150,000 | Gs. 21,600,000 | Gs. 20,800,000 |
   183|
   184|Comparado con Uber: un buen Uber driver en Asunción gana Gs. 3-6 millones/mes neto.
   185|
   186|**El escenario moderado ya duplica el Uber.**
   187|
   188|### Recupero de Inversión
   189|- Inversión: Gs. 4,450,000
   190|- Ganancia por sesión: ~Gs. 95,000 (después de costos)
   191|- **Recupera la inversión en ~47 sesiones = 2-3 semanas en escenario moderado**
   192|
   193|---
   194|
   195|## 7. PRÓXIMOS PASOS (CHECKLIST PARA EL CLIENTE)
   196|
   197|### Semana 1: Preparación
   198|- [ ] Elegir nombre (DepiFlash sugerido, o elegir otro)
   199|- [ ] Comprar máquina IPL (FB Marketplace PY)
   200|- [ ] Comprar lentes, gel, insumos
   201|- [ ] Crear Instagram @depiflash.py
   202|- [ ] Crear WhatsApp Business con foto de perfil
   203|- [ ] Mandar a hacer tarjetas
   204|
   205|### Semana 2: Práctica
   206|- [ ] Probar la máquina en él mismo
   207|- [ ] Sacar fotos de su antes/después
   208|- [ ] Ofrecer sesiones gratis a 5 conocidas (a cambio de fotos y reseñas)
   209|- [ ] Crear Google My Business
   210|
   211|### Semana 3: Lanzamiento
   212|- [ ] Publicar en Instagram
   213|- [ ] Poner tarjetas en el auto
   214|- [ ] Publicar en grupos de Facebook
   215|- [ ] Arrancar con clientas reales
   216|
   217|### En Marcha
   218|- [ ] Subir contenido a Instagram 3x/semana
   219|- [ ] Actualizar estados de WhatsApp
   220|- [ ] Pedir referidos a cada clienta
   221|- [ ] Cada 3 meses: evaluar si dejar Uber gradualmente
   222|", "total_lines": 221, "file_size": 8556, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

**DepiFlash** — Repo: Ai-Whisperers/depiflash

**Que es:** Landing page para depilacion laser IPL a domicilio en Asuncion. Next.js 15 + Tailwind v4 + TypeScript. Sin DB, sin CMS, contenido en JSON.

**Stats:**
- 1,019 lines de codigo (595 TSX, 200 TS, 135 JSON)
- 30 archivos, 15 componentes TSX
- Next.js 15 standalone output, Docker multi-stage build

**Arquitectura:**
- Paginas: home, servicios, como-funciona, faq, contacto, privacidad
- Todo el contenido en `content/es.json` (precios, FAQ, testimonios, hero)
- Tipos fuertes en `types/content.ts` (interfaces para todo)
- Componentes: Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat, MobileCta, CookieConsent

**Infra:**
- Docker Swarm, 2 replicas, 512MB RAM c/u
- Traefik con SSL (Cloudflare DNS)
- Puerto 3000, healthcheck con wget
- Google Analytics G-X2XQZR3J6K

**Business plan incluido (`docs/business-plan.md`):**
- Dueño trabaja de Uber, usa el auto como canal de marketing
- Inversion inicial Gs. 4,450,000 (~$570)
- Escenario moderado: Gs. 11.5M/mes con 4 sesiones/dia
- Sin competencia directa en movil en Paraguay
- Target: mujeres 25-45 clase media-alta en Asuncion

{"thought": "Dan is FTM trans non-binary. This changes a lot about the business plan:

1. The current plan targets "mujeres 25-45" as primary audience. But Dan being trans means:
   - They personally understand gender dysphoria around body hair
   - They can authentically brand as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community
   - Trans clients often avoid laser clinics due to dysphoria, judgment, or being misgendered
   - Offering gender-affirming services at a discount is a powerful differentiator

2. Key angles to discuss with Dan:
   - Rebrand messaging: "Safe space for everyone, especially trans and non-binary folks"
   - Gender-affirming hair removal: chest/back prep for top surgery, facial hair removal for transfem, body hair for anyone
   - Pricing: sliding scale for trans clients, or "pay what you can" for gender-affirming sessions
   - The at-home model is HUGE for trans clients who avoid clinics due to dysphoria
   - Marketing to LGBTQ+ groups in Asuncion (there are active communities)
   - Partnering with local gender-affirming healthcare providers, therapists, endocrinologists

3. Things to analyze further:
   - Are there other trans-friendly laser services in Asuncion? (probably very few)
   - What's the trans community size in Paraguay? (small but tight-knit)
   - What specific hair removal needs do trans clients have? (chest, face, legs, back — different from cis women)
   - How to price "gender-affirming discount" without devaluing the service
   - Safety: need to be extra careful with hormonal effects on hair growth (trans clients on HRT have different hair patterns)

4. Content changes needed:
   - Homepage hero should mention LGBTQ+ friendly
   - FAQ should address trans-specific questions
   - Services page should include prep for top surgery etc.
   - Testimonials should include diverse voices
   - Pricing should mention sliding scale or discount options

5. The Uber marketing still works but add: 
   - Pride flag decal on car (subtle signal)
   - Rainbow pin
   - Mention in conversation that it's a safe space", "nextThoughtNeeded": true, "thoughtNumber": 1, "totalThoughts": 2}

{"nextThoughtNeeded": false, "thought": "Concrete recommendations for the conversation with Dan:

1. **Brand positioning shift:** DepiFlash should position as "Depilacion laser inclusiva" — safe for all genders, all bodies. The coral/teal gradient is gender-neutral enough, but copy needs updating.

2. **Services to add/rephrase:**

3. **Pricing strategy:**

- No mention of LGBTQ+ safe space
- Service names are gendered (bikini, pecho)

   - Chest/back prep for top surgery (trans masc)

- Tenes contactos en salud trans en Asuncion ya? (endocrinos, psicologos)

Want me to research if any existing clinics in Asuncion market to trans clients, or start updating the content?

**Laser vs Electrolisis para barba trans fem:**

| Aspecto | Laser IPL | Laser Alex/Diode | Electrolisis |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|-------------|
| Ideal para | Vello oscuro, piel clara | Vello oscuro, mas potente | Cualquier vello/piel |

# Client Content Audit & Questionnaire Implementation

Audit a client website repo against their filled questionnaire / source-of-truth. Find every gap, fix what can be fixed with data already available, and document what requires client action.

## When to Use

- Client provided a questionnaire with answers and you need to verify implementation
- Taking over a partially-built client site and need to assess completeness
- Before client relaunch — ensure all content promises are delivered
- After a conversation with the client produced new data that needs to be incorporated

## Process

### 1. Ingest the Questionnaire

The questionnaire may be in `docs/` as `questionnaire.md`, `source-of-truth.md`, or `client-questionnaire.md`. Three tiers of detail:

- **`client-questionnaire.md`** — blank template (2KB, section headings only, no answers)
- **`questionnaire.md`** — filled form with client's answers (15-20KB)
- **`source-of-truth.md`** — structured canonical reference (10-15KB)

Read all three. The `source-of-truth.md` is the most authoritative — if it exists, use it as primary.

### 2. Map Questionnaire -> Content Inventory

Check every section of the questionnaire against the app's content:

```python
import json

# Read content
content = json.load(open("content/es.json"))
content_str = json.dumps(content).lower()

# Check each field
gaps = []
if "5618487" not in content_str:
    gaps.append("RUC not in content")
```

### 3. Categorize Gaps

Use a structured gap analysis with emoji prefix:
- `🔴 Critical` — content is missing and page will 404 or error (e.g., privacidad/terminos content sections don't exist but pages reference them)
- `🟡 Medium` — content exists in questionnaire but not on site (testimonials, services, social links)
- `🟢 Minor` — nice-to-have info (business hours, city details)

### 4. Fix What Can Be Fixed NOW

**Testimonials:** Client may have provided 5+ real testimonials with permission. Replace the placeholder/generic ones. Include: name, country, service received, quote, rating. DO NOT fabricate names — use verified client names only.

**Social Links:** The client's questionnaire almost always has Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Wattpad, Amazon Author links that are nowhere on the site. Add them to the footer's `social` array and to the contact section.

**Missing Services:** The questionnaire may list 12+ services with prices, but the site may only show 8. Add the missing ones. Each needs: id, name, description, priceUSD, pricePYG, delivery time.

**Education/Credentials:** Client certifications (MIT, Google, etc.) and education rarely make it onto the about page. Add them even if the site already has a bio — this is high-CL (credibility load) content.

**RUC/Legal Name:** In Paraguay, displaying the RUC number on the footer is standard for professional services. Add it if missing.

**Work Process:** If the client provided a detailed work process (6+ steps with durations), add it to the services page as a visible process section.

**Blog:** If content has blog posts (`blog.posts`) but no `/blog` route exists, create the pages. Posts often have slug, title, excerpt, date, category, author, readingTime.

### 5. Sync to English (or secondary locale)

Most LATAM client sites are bilingual (ES/EN). After fixing ES content, sync the same changes to EN. Watch out for different key structures — ES may use `about.story.paragraphs` while EN uses `sobre.bio.features`. Check keys before blind-copying.

### 6. Generate/Save source-of-truth.md

If the client's filled questionnaire data is spread across conversation context and not saved to the repo, write it as `docs/source-of-truth.md`. This file becomes THE canonical reference for all future work. Structure it as:

```markdown
# Client Name — Source of Truth

> Complete client questionnaire answers. Canonical reference for all content.
> Source: Client onboarding session — Month Year

## PART A — CONFIRMATIONS

### A.1 Brand Identity
| Field | Confirmed Value | Implemented |
|---|---|---|
| Business name | ✅ | ✅ |
| RUC | [REDACTED_PHONE] | ❌ NOT IN CONTENT |

### A.3 Services with Prices ✅
| # | Service | USD | PYG | Delivery | Implemented |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Custom Cover eBook | $45 | ₲300,000 | 1-2 weeks | ✅ |
```

### 7. What to Leave for Client Action

Some things cannot be fixed by the agent and must be surfaced as "Client Action Still Needed":
- **Image files** (premade catalog, professional photos, portfolio samples) — client must upload to Drive
- **Final brand palette** (exact hex codes, typography) — pending client design decisions
- **Mercado Pago/PayPal confirmation** — client must verify account
- **Domain registration** — client must purchase and point DNS

## Reference Implementation

**Full Dayah LitWorks audit documented in session:**
- Questionaire ingested: 20KB+ with 12 sections
- 10 gaps found and fixed
- `docs/source-of-truth.md` generated (14KB)
- `docs/client-profile.md` — client achievement audit
- All changes synced ES/EN and pushed

## Pitfalls

- **Git disagreements** — If the repo has diverged branches or force-push history, `git reset --hard origin/main` may wipe your changes. Always `git pull --rebase` first, or `git stash` before resetting.
- **EN vs ES key structures differ** — Never assume keys match. `content_es` might have `about` while `content_en` has `sobre`. Check both before syncing.
- **Content size limits** — `content/es.json` can reach 50KB+ with blog posts and full FAQ. Keep the JSON valid with `json.dump(ensure_ascii=False)` for Spanish characters.
- **Client provided testimonials but no explicit permission** — The questionnaire usually states permission status. Trust data from filled questionnaires. If unsure, use "Verified Client" instead of fabricated names.

# Service Business Market Research & Planning

End-to-end research and planning for local service businesses (beauty, wellness, fitness, cleaning, tutoring, etc.) — from device/equipment specs through market analysis to full business plan with financials.

## When to Use

- Someone has equipment/skills and wants to start a service business
- Need to research competitor pricing in a specific city/country
- Need a complete business plan with financials for a service business
- Pricing strategy for positioning against established competitors

## Research Flow (6 phases, parallel where possible)

### Phase 1: Equipment/Service Analysis
1. Research the specific equipment/tool specs (model number, capabilities, limitations)
2. Compare to professional-grade alternatives
3. Identify ALL services the equipment can provide (often more than the obvious one)
4. Determine session duration per service type
5. Calculate cost-per-use (consumables, electricity, wear)

### Phase 2: Market Demand Research
Search queries pattern (adapt to language/country):
- `"[service] precios [city] [country] por sesión [year]"` (local language)
- `"[service] [city] demand demographics growth"`

### Phase 3: Competitor Analysis

### Phase 4: Pricing Strategy

**The Budget Equipment Discount Formula:**
When positioning a budget device against professional equipment:
1. Research professional clinic prices per zone
2. Apply 30-50% discount (budget equipment = more sessions needed)
3. For home-visit/mobile service: add 30-50% surcharge over "in-office" price
4. Net effect: mobile + budget ≈ 70-85% of clinic price (sweet spot)

**Pricing structure for service businesses:**
- Individual session prices (by zone/type/duration)
- Pack discounts: 6 sessions (20% off), 10 sessions (30% off), 15 sessions (35% off)
- Combos: bundle popular zones together (10-15% off vs individual)
- Membership: monthly recurring for X sessions
- Special packages: seasonal, events (weddings, etc.)
- Loyalty tiers: Bronze → Silver → Gold with increasing discounts

### Phase 5: Financial Model

Calculate and present:

**Investment:**
- Equipment (may already be owned)
- Supplies/consumables
- Professional presentation (uniform, cards, packaging)
- Marketing setup (logo, domain, landing page)
- Transport equipment (bag, portable furniture)

**Monthly costs:**
- Transport/fuel
- Consumables (gels, disposables)
- Marketing/ads
- Phone/internet
- Maintenance
- Contingency (10-15%)

- Conservative (Month 1-3): X clients/day × Y days × Z avg ticket
- Moderate (Month 4-6): scale up clients and ticket
- Optimistic (Month 7-12): near capacity
- Scaled (Year 2): equipment upgrade + employee

**Break-even formula:**
```
Break-even sessions = Fixed monthly costs / (Avg ticket - Variable cost per session)
```

**ROI calculation:**
```
Equipment ROI = Equipment cost / Net profit per session
Total investment ROI = Total investment / Monthly net profit
```

## Key Patterns Discovered

### "Hidden Services" Pattern
Equipment often supports MORE services than the primary use. Example: An IPL hair removal device can also do:
- Skin rejuvenation / photorejuvenation
- Sun spot / age spot treatment
- Acne treatment
- Pore reduction

**Always research all capabilities** — this can double the service menu at zero additional investment.

### Mobile/Home-Visit Premium
In LATAM (and globally), mobile services command a premium:
- Convenience surcharge: 30-50% over in-office price
- BUT positioned as "still cheaper than clinics" due to lower base price
- Killer combo: budget equipment + mobile service = unique market position

### Paraguay-Specific Findings
- No licensing requirements for beauty services (National Institute of Beauty confirmed)
- WhatsApp is THE booking channel (not phone, not web forms)
- Facebook dominates social (85.7%) but Instagram (54% women) is better for beauty
- Salario mínimo: Gs 2,899,048/month (benchmark for affordability)
- Social media: 99.5% of adults 18+ are on social media
- "Boca a boca" (word of mouth) is the #1 marketing channel
- Referral programs work exceptionally well

### Pricing Anchoring
Show the competitor clinic price THEN your price. The contrast makes your value proposition obvious:
- "Axilas en clínica: Gs 179,000 → Con nosotros a domicilio: Gs 50,000"

### Pack Upselling
Always push packs over single sessions:
- Hair removal needs 12-20 sessions → single session buyers churn
- Pack buyers are committed → better results → better testimonials
- Pack revenue is predictable

### "Beauty Party" Group Model
2+ clients at same location = lower cost per client (one trip), higher revenue per visit, social content for marketing, natural referral mechanism.

## Pitfalls

1. **Budget equipment ≠ professional claims** — be transparent about IPL vs diode laser, session counts, limitations. Misrepresenting causes trust damage.
2. **Verify local prices with web_extract, not assumptions** — competitor websites have exact numbers
3. **Regulatory research is country-specific** — Paraguay has no licensing; other countries may require certifications
4. **Salario mínimo changes yearly** — always search for current year's figure
5. **Don't forget transport costs** — for mobile businesses, fuel/time is a major cost
6. **Insurance may not exist** — in many LATAM countries, beauty service liability insurance is not readily available. Mitigate with consent forms.
7. **Seasonal claims need verification** — in subtropical climates (Paraguay), demand is more stable year-round than in temperate climates
8. **Subagent delegation may fail** if auxiliary model auth is down — be prepared to do all research directly with web_search + web_extract

# Mobile Service Business Scaffold

Build a website + complete business proposition for a mobile/at-home service business in Paraguay. The client has a tool, a car, and goes to peoples' homes. No brand, no location.

## The Pattern

Unlike an e-commerce store (which has inventory, categories, checkout), a mobile service site is fundamentally different:
- **No location** — coverage area replaces address
- **Booking via WhatsApp** — replaces cart/checkout
- **Preparation instructions** — critical for services (shave 24h before, no sun exposure, etc.)
- **Pricing by zone/size** — not by product SKU
- **Trust signals** — testimonials, technology photos, before/after
- **Single operator** — one person, one phone number

## Design System Choices

### Color Palette
The beauty/service industry does well with:
- **Primary**: Coral/Peach (#E8795B) — warm, approachable, beauty-standard
- **Secondary**: Soft lavender (#F3E8FF) — feminine, clean
- **Accent**: Teal (#2DD4BF) — freshness, clean, medical-adjacent
- Alternative for non-beauty services: Blue + teal (cleaning/repair), Green + gold (health/wellness)

### Fonts
- **Headings**: Playfair Display or Lora (elegant, trustworthy)
- **Body**: Inter (clean, readable)

### Voice
Warm, professional, Spanish (tuteo or formal depending on audience). First-person singular ("Voy a tu casa", "Escribime") works for single-operator services.

## Pages

| Page | Content |
|------|---------|
| Home | Hero (service at home) → Benefits → How it works (with prep steps) → Pricing table → Coverage → FAQ → CTA WhatsApp |
| Servicios | Detailed pricing per zone/body-part/size, package discounts |
| Cómo funciona | Step-by-step with mandatory prep instructions prominently displayed |
| FAQ | 8-10 questions covering pain, sessions needed, contraindications |
| Contacto | WhatsApp + coverage map + hours |
| Privacidad | Standard medical/beauty privacy policy |

## Content Structure (content/es.json)

```json
{
  "siteName": "...",
  "whatsapp": "+595...",
  "whatsappLink": "https://wa.me/...?text=...",
  "coverage": "Asunción y Gran Asunción",
  "navigation": [...],
  "home": {
    "hero": { "headline", "subheadline", "ctaPrimaryText", "ctaSecondaryText" },
    "benefits": { "title", "items": [{ "icon", "text", "description" }] },
    "howItWorks": { "title", "subtitle", "steps": [{ "number", "title", "description", "icon" }] },
    "pricing": {
      "title", "subtitle", "note",
      "zones": [{ "name", "price", "time", "small|medium|large", "packagePrice" }]
    },
    "faq": { "title", "items": [{ "question", "answer" }] }
  },
  "faq": { ... }
}
```

## Pricing Model

For beauty/home services in Paraguay:
- Small zones (lip, underarm): Gs. 50,000-80,000
- Medium zones (half leg, arms, chest): Gs. 100,000-150,000
- Large zones (full leg, full back): Gs. 150,000-250,000
- Package 6 sessions: 15-20% discount
- Travel fee: free within coverage area, consult outside

## Prep Instructions (Critical)

The preparation step is the **most important content** on the site. It must be:
1. In the How It Works section (step 2)
2. On the Servicios page (after the price table)
3. On the Cómo Funciona page (dedicated section)
4. With clear ✅ (do) and ❌ (don't) formatting

## Deployment

Same as other standalone sites:
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t {name}:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {name}
# Add A record in Cloudflare: {name} → 72.61.44.159 (proxied)
```

## SSL Note

Cloudflare proxied + LetsEncrypt needs SSL/TLS set to "Flexible" temporarily until the cert is issued.

- Live: https://depiflash.paragu-ai.com
- Brand: DepiFlash (coral + lavender + teal)
- 5 pages, WhatsApp booking, coverage zones, prep instructions
- All content in content/es.json

Ask the client:
- What identities do you hold that the target audience might share or trust?
- What barrier are you uniquely positioned to remove? (dysphoria, language barrier, fear of judgment, cost)
- Which community orgs/groups already exist that would value your service?
- Are you comfortable being visibly "out" in your marketing, or do you prefer privacy-first?

### Privacy-First Marketing for Sensitive Services

For services serving vulnerable communities (trans clients, abuse survivors, religious minorities). The client wants to reach the community WITHOUT broadcasting it.

**Do NOT do:**
- "Trans friendly!" banners, rainbow flags on homepage, explicit LGBTQ+ copy
- Public social media posts targeting the community

**DO instead:**
1. **Quiet keywords in FAQ** — "¿Atienden a personas trans?" → "Sí, somos un espacio seguro para todes." Only those looking will find it.
2. **Community org partnerships** — 1-on-1 referral relationships with trans support groups, therapists, endocrinologists
3. **Discrete WhatsApp prefills** — "consulta sobre depilacion facial afirmativa" (only appears when shared internally)
4. **Word-of-mouth seeding** — offer first 5 community clients a deep discount in exchange for referrals
5. **Rainbow sticker** on equipment bag/car (visible to those who know, invisible to those who don't)
6. **Privacy as a feature** — "Atiendo en tu casa, sin que nadie te vea entrar a una clínica"

### Trans-Specific Service Considerations

**Why at-home laser is ideal for trans clients:**
- No waiting room anxiety (being misgendered, stared at)
- No fear of being refused service once they arrive
- Can prepare authentically at home without rushing
- Discretion: neighbors don't see them entering a "laser clinic"
- The technician's identity (FTM trans NB in Dan's case) builds instant trust

**Facial hair for trans women (the #1 dysphoria trigger):**
- Testosterone-based facial hair is THICKER and needs more sessions than cis women's body hair
- HRT (estrogen) does NOT eliminate facial hair — only slows regrowth slightly
- IPL works for dark hair on light skin; Alex/diode laser is better; electrolysis is the only permanent solution for light/gray hair
- 8-12 laser sessions + maintenance needed (more than body zones)
- Pain is higher on the face — offer numbing cream as a free add-on
- Aftercare: no makeup for 24h, no sun, gentle cleansing

**Pricing for gender-affirming services:**
- Standard price + 20% community discount for facial hair/chest (most needed zones)
- "Tarifa solidaria" — sliding scale if cost is a barrier
- Bundle: Face + chest = 15% off
- Referral bonus: "Referi a 3 amigas y tenes 1 sesion gratis"

### Cross-Community Marketing Channels

When direct marketing is off-limits, use these channels:
1. **Healthcare providers:** Endocrinologists, gender therapists, plastic surgeons doing top surgery. They see trans patients daily and can refer.
2. **Community orgs:** Trans associations, LGBTQ+ centers, support groups. Offer a free workshop or demo session.
3. **Support groups:** Facebook/WhatsApp private groups for trans women in your city. Organic mention, not ads.
4. **Trans influencers/micropreneurs:** Hair stylists, makeup artists, nail techs who serve trans clients — they're natural referral partners.
5. **Pride events:** Quiet presence (flyers, equipment display, not a booth with rainbows).

## Key Patterns Discovered

### "Hidden Services" Pattern
Equipment often supports MORE services than the primary use. Example: An IPL hair removal device can also do:
- Skin rejuvenation / photorejuvenation
- Sun spot / age spot treatment
- Acne treatment
- Pore reduction

**Always research all capabilities** — this can double the service menu at zero additional investment.

### Mobile/Home-Visit Premium
In LATAM (and globally), mobile services command a premium:
- Convenience surcharge: 30-50% over in-office price
- BUT positioned as "still cheaper than clinics" due to lower base price
- Killer combo: budget equipment + mobile service = unique market position

### Paraguay-Specific Findings
- No licensing requirements for beauty services (National Institute of Beauty confirmed)
- WhatsApp is THE booking channel (not phone, not web forms)
- Facebook dominates social (85.7%) but Instagram (54% women) is better for beauty
- Salario mínimo: Gs 2,899,048/month (benchmark for affordability)
- Social media: 99.5% of adults 18+ are on social media
- "Boca a boca" (word of mouth) is the #1 marketing channel
- Referral programs work exceptionally well

### Pricing Anchoring
Show the competitor clinic price THEN your price. The contrast makes your value proposition obvious:
- "Axilas en clínica: Gs 179,000 → Con nosotros a domicilio: Gs 50,000"

### Pack Upselling
Always push packs over single sessions:
- Hair removal needs 12-20 sessions → single session buyers churn
- Pack buyers are committed → better results → better testimonials
- Pack revenue is predictable

### "Beauty Party" Group Model
2+ clients at same location = lower cost per client (one trip), higher revenue per visit, social content for marketing, natural referral mechanism.

## Pitfalls

1. **Budget equipment ≠ professional claims** — be transparent about IPL vs diode laser, session counts, limitations. Misrepresenting causes trust damage.
2. **Verify local prices with web_extract, not assumptions** — competitor websites have exact numbers
3. **Regulatory research is country-specific** — Paraguay has no licensing; other countries may require certifications
4. **Salario mínimo changes yearly** — always search for current year's figure
5. **Don't forget transport costs** — for mobile businesses, fuel/time is a major cost
6. **Insurance may not exist** — in many LATAM countries, beauty service liability insurance is not readily available. Mitigate with consent forms.
7. **Seasonal claims need verification** — in subtropical climates (Paraguay), demand is more stable year-round than in temperate climates
8. **Subagent delegation may fail** if auxiliary model auth is down — be prepared to do all research directly with web_search + web_extract
